skill.name: hr-pro
skill.description: Professional HR assistant for hiring, onboarding, offboarding, leave, performance, policy drafting, and employee relations workflows.
skill.summary: Creates structured, compliance-aware HR templates and workflows with jurisdiction prompts and legal escalation notes.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, HR acronyms, and process terminology as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review of SKILL.md found no executable code, network access, filesystem manipulation, secret access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Code Spans
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code spans for a resource filename, template placeholders, and collaborator names. No command is executed, and no shell invocation syntax is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible as Markdown formatting in SKILL.md and is not part of any script or runnable source file. There is no evidence of command construction, interpolation, or execution.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in prose about HR scope, legal disclaimers, hiring, and job descriptions. The file does not implement hashing, encryption, token handling, or credential processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is plain instructional text for HR deliverables. No cryptographic API, algorithm choice, or security-sensitive data flow appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts are ordinary HR workflow language about leave, compliance, documentation, interview panels, and data minimization. The skill does not request host information or instruct the agent to inspect the runtime system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines describe HR policy and documentation practices. No commands, host enumeration steps, environment probes, or network discovery behavior are present.

content.value_statement: HR teams need consistent templates that account for policy, privacy, and jurisdiction differences. This skill produces structured HR drafts with assumptions, checklists, communications, and escalation notes.
actual_capabilities: Drafts job descriptions, structured interview questions, rubrics, scorecards, and candidate communications.
Creates onboarding and offboarding checklists with owners, timelines, and communication drafts.
Builds PTO and leave policy drafts with jurisdiction prompts and assumptions.
Produces performance review packets, competency matrices, and PIP templates.
Generates employee relations intake forms, investigation plans, notes formats, and findings memo outlines.
Adds legal and privacy notes that prompt review by qualified local counsel.
limitations: It does not provide legal advice or replace qualified employment counsel.
It needs jurisdiction, company size, employment type, and policy constraints for specific guidance.
It does not verify current labor law or benefits rules without user-provided sources.
It should not receive unnecessary sensitive personal data about employees or candidates.

use_cases.title: Create a structured hiring packet
use_cases.description: Generate a job description, interview plan, scoring rubric, and candidate communication templates for a role.

use_cases.title: Prepare compliant employee transitions
use_cases.description: Build onboarding or offboarding checklists with access, payroll, benefits, manager tasks, and communication drafts.

use_cases.title: Document employee relations work
use_cases.description: Create intake forms, investigation plans, objective note formats, and findings memo structures.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a basic HR policy
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single policy draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a PTO policy for a company in {{Jurisdiction}}. Include assumptions, eligibility, request workflow, carryover rules, and legal review notes.

prompt_templates.title: Build an interview kit
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a structured interview kit for {{RoleTitle}} at {{CompanyName}} in {{Jurisdiction}}. Include competencies, questions, scoring anchors, and debrief guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Create an onboarding plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 30/60/90 onboarding plan for a remote {{RoleTitle}} in {{Department}}. Include outcomes, stakeholders, training, check-ins, and manager actions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an employee relations packet
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation workflow with higher legal sensitivity.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an employee relations packet for {{IssueType}} in {{Jurisdiction}}. Include intake questions, investigation plan, documentation standards, findings memo outline, and counsel escalation points.

output_examples.input: Draft a structured interview kit for a senior accountant in California.
output: Summary of role assumptions and jurisdiction notes.
Competency list with job-related behavioral and situational questions.
Five-point scoring rubric with evidence-based anchors.
Panel plan, debrief checklist, and candidate communication drafts.

output_examples.input: Create an onboarding plan for a remote customer success manager.
output: 30/60/90 day plan with outcomes and learning goals.
First-week schedule, access checklist, and stakeholder map.
Manager check-in cadence and feedback loop recommendations.

output_examples.input: Prepare a PIP template for a sales manager with objective measures.
output: Objective expectations, support actions, timelines, and review checkpoints.
Legal and privacy notes for local counsel review before use.

best_practices: Provide jurisdiction, company size, employment type, and internal policy constraints before requesting final drafts.
Use job-related criteria, objective evidence, and standardized scoring for hiring and performance work.
Have qualified local counsel review high-risk actions before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to justify discriminatory hiring, discipline, or termination decisions.
Do not submit unnecessary medical, protected-class, or sensitive personal data.
Do not treat generated policies as final legal guidance without local review.

faq.question: Can this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides general HR information and templates, and it prompts review by qualified local counsel.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide jurisdiction, company size, employment type, work model, union status, and any internal policy constraints.

faq.question: Can it create hiring materials?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft job descriptions, structured interview questions, rubrics, scorecards, and candidate communications.

faq.question: Can it help with employee relations investigations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create intake forms, investigation plans, notes formats, and findings memo outlines.

faq.question: Does it handle international HR scenarios?
faq.answer: It can provide jurisdiction prompts and neutral templates, but country-specific rules require local counsel review.

faq.question: How should sensitive employee data be handled?
faq.answer: Share only the minimum necessary facts and avoid medical or protected-class details unless required.
